Based on the [Dialog History] provided, please answer the given [Question].

2. This question is being asked in the context of February 25, 2012.

[Question] Considering the intellectual discourse that transpired on February 14, 2010, to what neutral territory did Leonard propose to whisk Penny away for a romantic celebration of Valentine's Day concurrent with his conference attendance?
[Answer]
raw_source_record.dialog_bm25_dialog:
[{"role": "user", "content": "Context:
Coversation [February 8, 2010, Session #403]:
Speaker phone)says : Yeah, I miss you, too, sweetie. Listen, I got to go, but I’ll see you tonight? Okay. Bye-bye. Yeah, bye-bye. No, you hang up first. Hello?
Speaker Rajsays : Dude, I’m glad you finally got a girlfriend, but do you have to do all that lovey-dovey stuff in front of those of us who don’t?
Speaker Sheldonsays : Actually, he might have to. There’s an economic concept known as a positional good in which an object is only valued by the possessor because it’s not possessed by others. The term was coined in 1976 by economist Fred Hirsch to replace the more colloquial, but less precise neener-neener.
Speaker Howardsays : That’s not true. My happiness is not dependent on my best friend being miserable and alone.
Speaker Rajsays : Thank you.
Speaker Howardsays : Although, I’d be lying if I said it wasn’t a little bit of a perk.
Speaker Leonardsays : Who’s miserable and alone?
Speaker Rajsays : Me.
Speaker Leonardsays : Oh. I used to be like that. Then I got a girlfriend.
Speaker Sheldonsays : In pre-1976 terms, neener-neener.
Speaker Leonardsays : Hey, what are you and Bernadette doing for your first Valentine’s Day?
Speaker Howardsays : Yeah, I am pulling out all the stops. There’s a $39.95 lover’s special at P.F. Chang’s. Egg rolls, dumplings, bottomless wok, and you get your picture taken on the big marble horse out front.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Given that Saint Valentine was a third century Roman priest who was stoned and beheaded, wouldn’t a more appropriate celebration of the evening be taking one’s steady gal to witness a brutal murder?
Speaker Howardsays : I understand your point, but given a choice, Jews always go with Chinese food.
Speaker Rajsays : Well, if anyone’s interested, I’ll be spending this Valentine’s Day the same way I spend every Valentine’s Day. Buying a rotisserie chicken from the supermarket, taking it home, standing over the sink and eating it out of the package with my bare hands like an animal.
Speaker Leonardsays : Okay, so to sum up: one giant marble horse, one asinine comment, one lonely man and his chicken. And let’s see, who’s left? Oh, that’s right. My plans. Isn’t anyone going to ask?
Speaker Rajsays : Fine, tell us you’re going to have sex with Penny.
Speaker Leonardsays : That’s not what I was going to tell you.
Speaker Rajsays : It’s okay. I don’t mind hearing about your sex life. It’s his that bugs me.
Speaker Leonardsays : Guess who the university is sending to Switzerland to attend a conference and see the CERN supercollider on February 14?
Speaker Sheldonsays : Professor Norton, although, God knows why. He hasn’t published anything of note since he won that Nobel Prize.
Speaker Leonardsays : Actually, Professor Norton can’t make it. He threw his back out rock climbing.
Speaker Howardsays : I heard he threw his back out climbing on his new girlfriend.
Speaker Rajsays : The big-boobed weather girl on Channel 2?
Speaker Howardsays : That’s the one.
Speaker Leonardsays : In any case, they’re asking me to fill in for him.
Speaker Sheldonsays : In Switzerland or with the big-boobed weather girl?
Speaker Leonardsays : Switzerland. And I get to bring a guest!
Speaker Howardsays : Oh, man!
Speaker Rajsays : No way, dude!
Speaker Sheldonsays : This is incredible! I’m so happy, I’m not even going to question their judgment in picking you. I’m just going to run home and start packing.
Speaker Rajsays : Why wouldn’t you take Penny?
Speaker Leonardsays : I am taking Penny.
Speaker Rajsays : Oh. Well, then I anticipate an awkward situation when you get home.

Coversation [May 25, 2010, Session #467]:
Speaker Sheldonsays : In what universe is this low-pulp? Good morning, Penny.
Speaker Pennysays : What, do you have eyes in the back of your head?
Speaker Sheldonsays : When one gets beaten up every other day in school, one of necessity develops a keen sense of hearing. Incidentally, one can get beaten up in school simply by referring to oneself as one. I’m making English muffins. Would you like an English muffin?
Speaker Pennysays : Oh, thanks, I’m not hungry.
Speaker Sheldonsays : FYI, my noise-cancelling headphones proved ineffective last night.
Speaker Pennysays : Yeah, sorry about that.
Speaker Sheldonsays : As a native Texan, I must say I’ve never heard the phrase yee-haw used in quite that context.
Speaker Pennysays : Oh, God.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Oh, God. That I’ve heard on multiple occasions. In what universe is that lightly toasted? This has to be the worst day of my life. Good morning, Leonard.
Speaker Leonardsays : How many times have I asked you not to do that?
Speaker Sheldonsays : Counting this instance? 317.
Speaker Leonardsays : Where’s Penny?
Speaker Sheldonsays : She returned to her apartment. I presume to shower and vomit. Not necessarily in that order.
Speaker Leonardsays : I wonder why she didn’t say good-bye.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Are you expecting me to offer an explanation of human behaviour?
Speaker Leonardsays : I know. I just thought as an outsider, you might be able to provide a fresh perspective.
Speaker Sheldonsays : I have no difficulty believing you’re not butter.

Coversation [November 17, 2011, Session #709]:
Speaker Leonardsays : Hey, can I talk to you about something? It’s a little awkward.
Speaker Sheldonsays : I know what this is about. Given the professional standstill you’re at, you’re wondering if this is the appropriate time to abandon your research and focus on teaching. Yes. And if I may suggest, consider changing disciplines. Yeah, to the humanities, perhaps history. One of the advantages of teaching history is that you don’t have to create things, you know, you just have to remember stuff that happened and then parrot it back. You could have fun with that.
Speaker Leonardsays : Yeah, that’s not it. Stuart’s kind of interested in Amy.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Oh, of course he is. She’s very interesting. Did you know, when she was 14, she severed the webbing between her own toes?
Speaker Leonardsays : No. He wanted me to find out if you’d have a problem with him asking her out.
Speaker Sheldonsays : I’m not sure how to respond, Leonard. I don’t own Amy. Can’t own a person. At least not since? 1863. President Lincoln freed the? Slaves! Come on Leonard, if you’re going to teach history, these are the kind of facts you’ll have to know.
Speaker Leonardsays : You know what? Never mind. I’m going to tell him it’s okay to ask her out.
Speaker Sheldonsays : The question is moot. There is no way that Stuart, an impoverished peddler of picture books, would be at all appealing to Amy Farrah Fowler, a noted neurobiologist capable of performing surgery on her own feet with nothing but nitrous oxide from cans of whipped cream as anesthesia.
Speaker Leonardsays : All right, but just for the record, I checked in with you to see how you’d feel about it.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Fine. The record shall so reflect. Now, getting back to the problem at hand, what to do with a washed-up experimental physicist.
Speaker Leonardsays : I am not washed-up.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Oh, Leonard. Lots of people love you and want to help you, but they can’t until you admit the problem.

Coversation [May 3, 2012, Session #801]:
Speaker Pennysays : Amy, you look great.
Speaker Amysays : I know.
Speaker Leonardsays : Where’d you get a beer?
Speaker Rajsays : From that happy young couple over there with all the tattoos. Beautiful story, they’re in rival drug gangs, and they’re getting married. Shh, no one can know.
Speaker Amysays : Look at all these people in love. It kind of gets you thinking, doesn’t it?
Speaker Sheldonsays : It does, indeed. Leonard, is it awkward being here with Penny given that you recently proposed to her?
Speaker Howardsays : You proposed to Penny?
Speaker Leonardsays : I don’t want to talk about it.
Speaker Rajsays : Where did he pop the question? What did you say?
Speaker Leonardsays : She said no, can we drop it now?
Speaker Pennysays : It wasn’t a real proposal.
Speaker Bernadettesays : Why wasn’t it a real proposal?
Speaker Sheldonsays : He asked her during coitus.
Speaker Howardsays : Did you get down on one knee or were you already there?
Speaker Bernadettesays : Howard, don’t talk like that on your wedding day.
Speaker Howardsays : I’m sorry, Ma… Bernadette. Ma… Burna… You’re ma Bernadette.
Speaker Pennysays : Good move telling Sheldon.
Speaker Leonardsays : What, I can’t propose? I can’t talk to my friends? Is there anything else I’m not allowed to do?
Speaker Amysays : All right, that’s enough. Today is not about you two. Today is about Howard and Bernadette and me.
Speaker Registrarsays : Folks, can I have your attention. It’s five o’clock, we’re going to be able to take three more couples. The rest of you will have to come back on Monday.
Speaker Bernadettesays : Oh, no.
Speaker Howardsays : I got this. Excuse me, but is there any way you could squeeze us in? See, I’m an astronaut and I’m leaving for Russia on Sunday so I can take a Soyuz rocket to the International Space Station.
Speaker Registrarsays : Yeah, me, too. I’ll see you there.
Speaker Bernadettesays : I can’t believe we’re not going to get married.
Speaker Amysays : Excuse me, I’m going to go see if the couple at the front of the line needs a maid of honour.

Coversation [February 25, 2012, Session #768]:
Speaker Amysays : He showed up in the middle of the night with his bongos. I foolishly thought it was some sort of musical booty call.
Speaker Leonardsays : Poor guy. He must have been exhausted. Sheldon like to sleep while Leonard play the bongo!
Speaker Amysays : That was kind of uncalled for.
Speaker Leonardsays : No, it was called for.
Speaker Sheldonsays : What’s going on?
Speaker Pennysays : All right, Sheldon, this craziness has gone on long enough. Please come home so I can cut your hair.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Penny, you’re not trained. You’re not licensed. Most importantly, you don’t have access to my haircut records.
Speaker Pennysays : All right, honey, look, we’ve known each other for a long time now, right? I’ve taken you to Disneyland, I kicked a bully in the nuts for you, I sing you Soft Kitty when you’re sick, you’ve even saw me naked once.
Speaker Leonardsays : I’m sorry, duh, what?
Speaker Pennysays : It was a long story. Anyway, Sheldon, I promise I know what I’m doing. Please let me cut your hair.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Amy, what do you think?
Speaker Amysays : There’s not a hair on my body I wouldn’t let this woman trim.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Fine, let’s go. Thank you for letting me sleep on your couch.
Speaker Amysays : There’s only so many times a woman can say how about the bed?
Speaker Leonardsays : What’s this about Sheldon seeing you naked?
Speaker Sheldonsays : Oh, relax. It was just her bottom and her breasts.

raw_source_record.dialog_embedder_dialog:
[{"role": "user", "content": "Context:
Coversation [April 1, 2011, Session #615]:
Speaker Sheldonsays : Thank you, Penny.
Speaker Pennysays : No problem. So, Leonard, I think it’s interesting you didn’t call your girlfriend to come get you.
Speaker Leonardsays : Uh, I kind of told her I was working.
Speaker Pennysays : So you lied to her. Also interesting.
Speaker Leonardsays : Yeah, she doesn’t really understand the whole Warcraft adventure-role-playing thing.
Speaker Pennysays : Well, doesn’t matter if she gets it, as long as she’s pretty.
Speaker Howardsays : This one’s funny, Leonard. How come you couldn’t make it work with her?
Speaker Pennysays : So did you at least get Sheldon’s fake stuff back?
Speaker Sheldonsays : No. We failed in our noble quest.
Speaker Pennysays : How come?
Speaker Sheldonsays : Todd Zarnecki was mean.
Speaker Pennysays : All right. Hang on.
Speaker Leonardsays : What are you doing?
Speaker Pennysays : I’m gonna show you how we finish a quest in Nebraska. Oh. Beyonce.  This ain’t no Beyonce.

Coversation [March 26, 2008, Session #71]:
Speaker door)says : Oh hey Penny, wow, look at you, all ready for your showcase, you look great.
Speaker Pennysays : Thanks. I just wanted to come by and wish you guys luck with your symposium.
Speaker Leonardsays : Oh, well, thankyou.
Speaker Pennysays : You know, I got to tell you, a lot of friends would let their friend go alone, but that’s not who you are, you are the kind of guy who stands by a friend when… when he has a symposium to go to.
Speaker Leonardsays : I don’t know what to say.
Speaker Pennysays : It’s okay, Leonard
Speaker Leonardsays : Oh, okay, alright, good.
Speaker (arriving)says : Oh boy, group hug.
Speaker Pennysays : Uh-huh!
Speaker Howardsays : Uh-huh?
Speaker Pennysays : Uh-huh!
Speaker Howardsays : Okay. So what’s up?
Speaker Sheldonsays : Well, uh, Penny is on her way to perform in a one night showcase production of Rent, which we are unable to attend because we are going to a symposium on molecular positronium, given by Dr Emile Farminfarmian.
Speaker Howardsays : Wait a minute, Farminfarmian is speaking and you’re Bogarding the symposium.
Speaker Leonardsays : Howard, I’m sorry… we’re… we’re
Speaker Howardsays : No, no, you’re quark-blocking us.
Speaker Leonardsays : I don’t know what to say.
Speaker Howardsays : Wow.
Speaker Leonardsays : Howard, listen…
Speaker Howardsays : No, it’s okay, it’s your Millenium Falcon, you and Chewbacca do whatever you want to do. Me and Princess Leia here will find some other way to spend the evening.
Speaker Pennysays : Howard, wait. Sheldon, I think we should tell them.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Okay, sure. I don’t see a problem with that.
Speaker Pennysays : There’s no symposium, Leonard lied to me, isn’t that right Leonard.
Speaker Leonardsays : Well… I don’t know what to say.
Speaker Pennysays : It’s okay, I do, look, Leonard is helping Sheldon through a family crisis, he made up the whole story about the symposium with Dr Farmin..farm…ian
Speaker Sheldonsays : Good for you.
Speaker Pennysays : Hah, yeah! Because he didn’t want Sheldon to be embarrassed, and there is nothing to be embarrassed okay, every family in America has a relative holed up in a garage somewhere huffing paint thinner.
Speaker ear)says : No, I’m lost too, I think she skipped a step.
Speaker Pennysays : No, look, Sheldon’s cousin Leo escaped rehab, and he’s in a Motel 8 at Long Beach, the whole family’s going out for an intervention. Leonard is driving Sheldon down there to help him through this because he’s such a good man.
Speaker Leonardsays : Oh, another hug, thank you.
Speaker Pennysays : Alright you guys, good luck.
Speaker Leonardsays : Thanks Penny.
Speaker Howardsays : Yeah, uh, break a leg.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Break a leg.
Speaker Howardsays : So, road trip to Long Beach.
Speaker Leonardsays : No, we’re not going to Long Beach.
Speaker Rajsays : Why not?
Speaker Leonardsays : Because Sheldon doesn’t have a drug addicted cousin Leopold.
Speaker Rajsays : Oh, too bad. I’ve always wanted to go to Long Beach.
Speaker Sheldonsays : It’s a very nice community. The Queen Mary is docked there, once the largest ocean liner in the world, it’s now a hotel and restaurant where they host a surprisingly gripping murder mystery dinner.
Speaker Rajsays : Sounds fun.
Speaker Howardsays : I’m game.
Speaker Rajsays : Shotgun.
Speaker Sheldonsays : No, no, no, Leonard gets nauseous unless he sits in front, and even then it’s iffy.
Speaker Leonardsays : Wait, are we really going to Long Beach?

Coversation [February 12, 2010, Session #410]:
Speaker Leonardsays : Hey, Sheldon? Listen, Penny is pretty sick and she’s not going to be able to go to Switzerland. So if you’re still interested, you’re welcome to come.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Great. I’ll start packing. In a minute.  Oh, look, it’s the cholermus.

Coversation [February 3, 2009, Session #238]:
Speaker Leonardsays : Okay, is everyone clear on the plan?
Speaker Howardsays : Yes. Koothrappali’s going to wet himself, I’m gonna throw up, Sheldon’s gonna run away, and you’re going to die. Shall we synchronize our watches?
Speaker Leonardsays : Guys, there are four of us and one of him.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Which means his triumph will be even larger. Minstrels will write songs about him.
Speaker Leonardsays : Okay, how about this? I’ll do the talking, you just stand behind me and try to look formidable.
Speaker Rajsays : I should’ve peed before we left.
Speaker Kurtsays : Yeah?
Speaker Leonardsays : Hi, Kurt.
Speaker Kurtsays : Lenny, right?
Speaker Leonardsays : I don’t really go by Lenny, but that’s okay. Um, you remember Sheldon, Howard and Raj.
Speaker Kurtsays : No. What do you want?
Speaker Sheldonsays : You don’t remember me? How could he not remember me?
Speaker Leonardsays : Sheldon, not now.
Speaker Sheldonsays : I remember him.
Speaker Leonardsays : Okay, here it is. Penny’s in kind of a financial jam, and the money that you owe her would go a long way to solving her problems.
Speaker Kurtsays : And she sent you to get it from me?
Speaker Leonardsays : No, no, she’s too proud to ask for the money. I, on the other hand, feel you should honour your debt.
Speaker Kurtsays : You do?
Speaker Leonardsays : Feel is a kind of a… it’s a strong word. Um, I just think it would be a nice gesture on your part.
Speaker Kurtsays : She’ll get it when she gets it.
Speaker Howardsays : Well, there you go. Problem solved.
Speaker Rajsays : A successful quest. Now let’s go find a gas station with a clean bathroom.
Speaker Leonardsays : No, the problem isn’t solved. He just blew us off.
Speaker Sheldonsays : I’ve got it. He didn’t remember me because the last time we met I was in a Halloween costume.
Speaker Howardsays : Come on, Leonard, let’s go
Speaker Leonardsays : No. You can leave if you want to. I’m gonna see this through.
Speaker Howardsays : Okay.
Speaker Leonardsays : I guess, technically, that was my fault.  I’m not leaving here without Penny’s money.
Speaker Kurtsays : What happened to your backup?
Speaker Leonardsays : I don’t need backup. I have right on my side. And I’m wearing cargo shorts under my pants.

Coversation [May 13, 2009, Session #300]:
Speaker Leonardsays : Sheldon, Sheldon?
Speaker Sheldonsays : I want a cookie, Meemaw.
Speaker Leonardsays : Sheldon, it’s me.
Speaker Sheldonsays : But Meemaw just made cookies.
Speaker Leonardsays : Listen, I don’t know if I can go on the expedition.
Speaker Sheldonsays : What?
Speaker Leonardsays : I don’t think I can go to the North Pole.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Okay, Leonard, I know you’re concerned about disappointing me but I want you to take comfort from the knowledge that my expectations of you are very low.
Speaker Leonardsays : Yeah, that’s very comforting.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Comforting is a part of leadership. It’s not a part I care for, but such is my burden.
Speaker Leonardsays : Terrific, it’s just that I don’t think Penny wants me to go.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Assuming that’s a valid reason not to go, which it isn’t, how do you know this? Did she say it?
Speaker Leonardsays : Not exactly. But she said she was gonna miss me and she gave me this.
Speaker Sheldonsays : What is it?
Speaker Leonardsays : It’s a blanket with sleeves.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Oh, that’s clever. Let me see if I understand this correctly. Her missing you is an emotional state you find desirable?
Speaker Leonardsays : Yes, obviously.
Speaker Sheldonsays : All right. Well, given that missing you is predicated on you leaving, logic dictates you must leave.
Speaker Leonardsays : Yes, okay, but I’m gonna be gone for three months. What if she doesn’t miss me that long and she meets someone else?
Speaker Sheldonsays : She does have a short attention span.
Speaker Leonardsays : So, I can’t go.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Leonard, you may be right. It appears that Penny secretly wants you in her life in a very intimate and carnal fashion.
Speaker Leonardsays : You really think so?
Speaker Sheldonsays : Of course not. Even in my sleep-deprived state, I’ve managed to pull off another one of my classic pranks. Bazinga!

supporting_material.dialog_context:
[{"role": "user", "content": "Context:
Coversation [February 8, 2010, Session #403]:
Speaker phone)says : Yeah, I miss you, too, sweetie. Listen, I got to go, but I’ll see you tonight? Okay. Bye-bye. Yeah, bye-bye. No, you hang up first. Hello?
Speaker Rajsays : Dude, I’m glad you finally got a girlfriend, but do you have to do all that lovey-dovey stuff in front of those of us who don’t?
Speaker Sheldonsays : Actually, he might have to. There’s an economic concept known as a positional good in which an object is only valued by the possessor because it’s not possessed by others. The term was coined in 1976 by economist Fred Hirsch to replace the more colloquial, but less precise neener-neener.
Speaker Howardsays : That’s not true. My happiness is not dependent on my best friend being miserable and alone.
Speaker Rajsays : Thank you.
Speaker Howardsays : Although, I’d be lying if I said it wasn’t a little bit of a perk.
Speaker Leonardsays : Who’s miserable and alone?
Speaker Rajsays : Me.
Speaker Leonardsays : Oh. I used to be like that. Then I got a girlfriend.
Speaker Sheldonsays : In pre-1976 terms, neener-neener.
Speaker Leonardsays : Hey, what are you and Bernadette doing for your first Valentine’s Day?
Speaker Howardsays : Yeah, I am pulling out all the stops. There’s a $39.95 lover’s special at P.F. Chang’s. Egg rolls, dumplings, bottomless wok, and you get your picture taken on the big marble horse out front.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Given that Saint Valentine was a third century Roman priest who was stoned and beheaded, wouldn’t a more appropriate celebration of the evening be taking one’s steady gal to witness a brutal murder?
Speaker Howardsays : I understand your point, but given a choice, Jews always go with Chinese food.
Speaker Rajsays : Well, if anyone’s interested, I’ll be spending this Valentine’s Day the same way I spend every Valentine’s Day. Buying a rotisserie chicken from the supermarket, taking it home, standing over the sink and eating it out of the package with my bare hands like an animal.
Speaker Leonardsays : Okay, so to sum up: one giant marble horse, one asinine comment, one lonely man and his chicken. And let’s see, who’s left? Oh, that’s right. My plans. Isn’t anyone going to ask?
Speaker Rajsays : Fine, tell us you’re going to have sex with Penny.
Speaker Leonardsays : That’s not what I was going to tell you.
Speaker Rajsays : It’s okay. I don’t mind hearing about your sex life. It’s his that bugs me.
Speaker Leonardsays : Guess who the university is sending to Switzerland to attend a conference and see the CERN supercollider on February 14?
Speaker Sheldonsays : Professor Norton, although, God knows why. He hasn’t published anything of note since he won that Nobel Prize.
Speaker Leonardsays : Actually, Professor Norton can’t make it. He threw his back out rock climbing.
Speaker Howardsays : I heard he threw his back out climbing on his new girlfriend.
Speaker Rajsays : The big-boobed weather girl on Channel 2?
Speaker Howardsays : That’s the one.
Speaker Leonardsays : In any case, they’re asking me to fill in for him.
Speaker Sheldonsays : In Switzerland or with the big-boobed weather girl?
Speaker Leonardsays : Switzerland. And I get to bring a guest!
Speaker Howardsays : Oh, man!
Speaker Rajsays : No way, dude!
Speaker Sheldonsays : This is incredible! I’m so happy, I’m not even going to question their judgment in picking you. I’m just going to run home and start packing.
Speaker Rajsays : Why wouldn’t you take Penny?
Speaker Leonardsays : I am taking Penny.
Speaker Rajsays : Oh. Well, then I anticipate an awkward situation when you get home.

Coversation [May 25, 2010, Session #467]:
Speaker Sheldonsays : In what universe is this low-pulp? Good morning, Penny.
Speaker Pennysays : What, do you have eyes in the back of your head?
Speaker Sheldonsays : When one gets beaten up every other day in school, one of necessity develops a keen sense of hearing. Incidentally, one can get beaten up in school simply by referring to oneself as one. I’m making English muffins. Would you like an English muffin?
Speaker Pennysays : Oh, thanks, I’m not hungry.
Speaker Sheldonsays : FYI, my noise-cancelling headphones proved ineffective last night.
Speaker Pennysays : Yeah, sorry about that.
Speaker Sheldonsays : As a native Texan, I must say I’ve never heard the phrase yee-haw used in quite that context.
Speaker Pennysays : Oh, God.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Oh, God. That I’ve heard on multiple occasions. In what universe is that lightly toasted? This has to be the worst day of my life. Good morning, Leonard.
Speaker Leonardsays : How many times have I asked you not to do that?
Speaker Sheldonsays : Counting this instance? 317.
Speaker Leonardsays : Where’s Penny?
Speaker Sheldonsays : She returned to her apartment. I presume to shower and vomit. Not necessarily in that order.
Speaker Leonardsays : I wonder why she didn’t say good-bye.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Are you expecting me to offer an explanation of human behaviour?
Speaker Leonardsays : I know. I just thought as an outsider, you might be able to provide a fresh perspective.
Speaker Sheldonsays : I have no difficulty believing you’re not butter.

Coversation [November 17, 2011, Session #709]:
Speaker Leonardsays : Hey, can I talk to you about something? It’s a little awkward.
Speaker Sheldonsays : I know what this is about. Given the professional standstill you’re at, you’re wondering if this is the appropriate time to abandon your research and focus on teaching. Yes. And if I may suggest, consider changing disciplines. Yeah, to the humanities, perhaps history. One of the advantages of teaching history is that you don’t have to create things, you know, you just have to remember stuff that happened and then parrot it back. You could have fun with that.
Speaker Leonardsays : Yeah, that’s not it. Stuart’s kind of interested in Amy.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Oh, of course he is. She’s very interesting. Did you know, when she was 14, she severed the webbing between her own toes?
Speaker Leonardsays : No. He wanted me to find out if you’d have a problem with him asking her out.
Speaker Sheldonsays : I’m not sure how to respond, Leonard. I don’t own Amy. Can’t own a person. At least not since? 1863. President Lincoln freed the? Slaves! Come on Leonard, if you’re going to teach history, these are the kind of facts you’ll have to know.
Speaker Leonardsays : You know what? Never mind. I’m going to tell him it’s okay to ask her out.
Speaker Sheldonsays : The question is moot. There is no way that Stuart, an impoverished peddler of picture books, would be at all appealing to Amy Farrah Fowler, a noted neurobiologist capable of performing surgery on her own feet with nothing but nitrous oxide from cans of whipped cream as anesthesia.
Speaker Leonardsays : All right, but just for the record, I checked in with you to see how you’d feel about it.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Fine. The record shall so reflect. Now, getting back to the problem at hand, what to do with a washed-up experimental physicist.
Speaker Leonardsays : I am not washed-up.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Oh, Leonard. Lots of people love you and want to help you, but they can’t until you admit the problem.

Coversation [May 3, 2012, Session #801]:
Speaker Pennysays : Amy, you look great.
Speaker Amysays : I know.
Speaker Leonardsays : Where’d you get a beer?
Speaker Rajsays : From that happy young couple over there with all the tattoos. Beautiful story, they’re in rival drug gangs, and they’re getting married. Shh, no one can know.
Speaker Amysays : Look at all these people in love. It kind of gets you thinking, doesn’t it?
Speaker Sheldonsays : It does, indeed. Leonard, is it awkward being here with Penny given that you recently proposed to her?
Speaker Howardsays : You proposed to Penny?
Speaker Leonardsays : I don’t want to talk about it.
Speaker Rajsays : Where did he pop the question? What did you say?
Speaker Leonardsays : She said no, can we drop it now?
Speaker Pennysays : It wasn’t a real proposal.
Speaker Bernadettesays : Why wasn’t it a real proposal?
Speaker Sheldonsays : He asked her during coitus.
Speaker Howardsays : Did you get down on one knee or were you already there?
Speaker Bernadettesays : Howard, don’t talk like that on your wedding day.
Speaker Howardsays : I’m sorry, Ma… Bernadette. Ma… Burna… You’re ma Bernadette.
Speaker Pennysays : Good move telling Sheldon.
Speaker Leonardsays : What, I can’t propose? I can’t talk to my friends? Is there anything else I’m not allowed to do?
Speaker Amysays : All right, that’s enough. Today is not about you two. Today is about Howard and Bernadette and me.
Speaker Registrarsays : Folks, can I have your attention. It’s five o’clock, we’re going to be able to take three more couples. The rest of you will have to come back on Monday.
Speaker Bernadettesays : Oh, no.
Speaker Howardsays : I got this. Excuse me, but is there any way you could squeeze us in? See, I’m an astronaut and I’m leaving for Russia on Sunday so I can take a Soyuz rocket to the International Space Station.
Speaker Registrarsays : Yeah, me, too. I’ll see you there.
Speaker Bernadettesays : I can’t believe we’re not going to get married.
Speaker Amysays : Excuse me, I’m going to go see if the couple at the front of the line needs a maid of honour.

Coversation [February 25, 2012, Session #768]:
Speaker Amysays : He showed up in the middle of the night with his bongos. I foolishly thought it was some sort of musical booty call.
Speaker Leonardsays : Poor guy. He must have been exhausted. Sheldon like to sleep while Leonard play the bongo!
Speaker Amysays : That was kind of uncalled for.
Speaker Leonardsays : No, it was called for.
Speaker Sheldonsays : What’s going on?
Speaker Pennysays : All right, Sheldon, this craziness has gone on long enough. Please come home so I can cut your hair.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Penny, you’re not trained. You’re not licensed. Most importantly, you don’t have access to my haircut records.
Speaker Pennysays : All right, honey, look, we’ve known each other for a long time now, right? I’ve taken you to Disneyland, I kicked a bully in the nuts for you, I sing you Soft Kitty when you’re sick, you’ve even saw me naked once.
Speaker Leonardsays : I’m sorry, duh, what?
Speaker Pennysays : It was a long story. Anyway, Sheldon, I promise I know what I’m doing. Please let me cut your hair.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Amy, what do you think?
Speaker Amysays : There’s not a hair on my body I wouldn’t let this woman trim.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Fine, let’s go. Thank you for letting me sleep on your couch.
Speaker Amysays : There’s only so many times a woman can say how about the bed?
Speaker Leonardsays : What’s this about Sheldon seeing you naked?
Speaker Sheldonsays : Oh, relax. It was just her bottom and her breasts.
